task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided Python function correctly calculates the median of a list, handles edge cases, and has clear and concise code.
trace: ** The `median` function first checks if the input list `nums` is empty and returns `None` immediately if it is, handling this edge case. If not empty, it sorts the list and calculates the middle index `mid`. If the length of the list is odd (`n % 2 == 1`), it returns the value at the middle index as a float. If the length is even, it calculates the average of the two middle values by adding them together and dividing by 2.0, also returning this value as a float.
